What is the factual difference?
Good question. Clean answer.
There are three real differences — structural, operational, and psychological.
1️⃣ Structural Difference
Absorbed (Single Repo)
site_builder/  becomes just a folder inside RODZAKI.github.io .
One .git  directory total.
One history.
One commit stream.
Cloning the repo gives you everything automatically.
Submodule (Nested Repo)
site_builder/  remains its own repository with its own history.
The parent repo only stores a pointer to a specific commit of that child repo.
Cloning the outer repo does NOT automatically give you the contents unless you run:
So it is operationally more complex.
2️⃣ Deployment Difference (Important for GitHub Pages)
git submodule update --init --recursive

Submodule

Submodules aren’t initialized

Absorbed
Cleaner
Simpler
Lower entropy
Fewer moving parts
Ideal if site_builder exists only to serve this site
Submodule
